title: Nursery Rhyme. LIII. Tales.
content:
There was a man, and he had naught,
            And robbers came to rob him;
        He crept up to the chimney pot,
            And then they thought they had him.

        But he got down on t'other side,
            And then they could not find him;
        He ran fourteen miles in fifteen days,
            And never look'd behind him.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. LIII. Tales."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
